author: Maya Chen
text: After 6 months building with Next.js, here's what nobody tells you about the App Router: caching is the feature AND the footgun. We cut our build times in half once we understood it.

author: @devjordan
text: hot take: most teams don't need a state management library. Next.js + URL params + server components covers 90% of cases. fight me

author: Priya Nair
text: We migrated our dashboard to Next.js and shipped a unified analytics feed in two weeks. Server components made the data layer almost boring. Boring is good.

author: @frontendfran
text: spent all day debugging hydration errors. turns out it was a Date() rendered on the server vs client. Next.js you owe me a coffee ☕

author: Tomás Rivera
text: Reminder: a fast dashboard is a product feature, not an engineering vanity metric. Our Next.js rebuild dropped p95 load to under 400ms and support tickets fell with it.

author: @sara_builds
text: shipped a side project this weekend with Next.js and Tailwind. 0 to deployed in 4 hours. the DX is genuinely unreal right now

author: Wei Zhang
text: Interviewing frontend engineers in 2026: I no longer ask about frameworks. I ask how they'd structure a Next.js project for a team of 20. The answers tell me everything.

author: @perfnerd
text: benchmarked 5 meta-frameworks this week. Next.js wins on ecosystem, loses on cold starts. pick your tradeoff carefully and stop chasing benchmarks

author: Aisha Bello
text: Mentorship moment: a junior asked why we use Next.js over plain React. My answer: because routing, data fetching, and rendering decisions should be conventions, not debates.

author: @codingcarlos
text: unpopular: server actions in Next.js are great until you need 3 of them on one form. then you miss your old API routes. mixed feelings honestly
